prompt: Cinematic photography, warm tungsten light. An old tailor's workshop, cluttered with fabric and thread. In the foreground, a tailor's mannequin wearing a finely constructed suit that is visibly, bizarrely over-fitted to one specific body — the jacket shoulders are uneven, one side padded higher, the chest panel curves around a particular belly, the sleeves torqued at an odd angle — every stitch capturing a specific person's exact imperfections. In the background, slightly out of focus, a second person is trying on an identical suit from a rack of copies — it pulls badly across the shoulders, bunches at the chest, clearly made for someone else entirely. The workshop is filled with measuring tape, chalk marks on paper, pattern pieces pinned to a board. Late evening light. Mood: obsessive precision that has become its own failure. Photorealistic, 35mm film aesthetic. No text, no labels, no numbers, no diagrams, no words visible anywhere in the image.
metaphor: A suit tailored to one body's exact imperfections fits nobody else — precision on training data, failure on new inputs.
